articles.title: Spider-Noir Trailer Unleashes Nicolas Cage Against Classic Villains—and a Deep-Cut Foe
articles.summary: A new trailer for Spider-Noir, starring Nicolas Cage as Ben Reilly, shows off fights with Sandman, Silvermane, and the obscure villain Megawatt. The series, set in the 1930s, follows a retired private investigator with spider powers who returns to heroism. It premieres May 25 on MGM Plus and May 27 on Prime Video, in both black-and-white and color. The trailer reassures fans that the show will embrace the fantastical elements of the comics.

articles.title: Carlos Alcaraz Withdraws from French Open Due to Wrist Injury, Opening Door for Rival Sinner
articles.summary: Two-time defending French Open champion Carlos Alcaraz announced he will miss the 2026 tournament due to a right wrist injury sustained at the Barcelona Open. The 22-year-old Spaniard, who won the Australian Open earlier this year, also withdrew from the Italian Open. His absence gives rival Jannik Sinner a chance to complete his career Grand Slam. Sinner expressed sadness but hoped Alcaraz would return for Wimbledon.

articles.title: Miles Morales Actor Claims Venom Game Was Canceled After Tony Todd's Death—But Reporter Disputes It
articles.summary: Nadji Jeter, the voice of Miles Morales, said in an interview that a standalone Venom game and DLC were planned but canceled following the death of actor Tony Todd, who voiced Venom. However, Bloomberg reporter Jason Schreier contradicted Jeter's account, calling it untrue. Leaked Insomniac documents from 2023 revealed a Venom game was in development, but no official announcement has been made. The studio is currently focused on Marvel's Wolverine.
